name: Cow milk, pasteurized, vitamin A + D added, 2% fat (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight (fluid milk; 100 g ≈ 96.9 mL at density 1.032 g/mL)

components.name: lactose
components.source: USDA FDC SR Legacy 'Milk, reduced fat, 2% milkfat, +vit A +vit D' (NDB 01079); total carbohydrate in fluid milk is essentially all lactose
components.evidence: DOMINANT carbon/energy source. Total sugars 4.8-5.1 g/100g, effectively all lactose (disaccharide glucose+galactose).

components.name: D-glucose (free)
components.source: FooDB FOOD00891; Fox & McSweeney, Dairy Chemistry (free glucose ~0.07-0.1 g/L)
components.evidence: Trace free monosaccharide from lactose turnover; ~10x below lactose.

components.name: D-galactose (free)
components.source: FooDB FOOD00891; Fox & McSweeney, Dairy Chemistry
components.evidence: Trace free monosaccharide; present at ~mg/L level.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Garnsworthy/CABI 'Variability of citric acid in cow milk' doi:10.5555/20123239825; classic bovine milk citrate 8-10 mM (~1.75 g/L)
components.evidence: Principal milk organic acid; 9.4 mM matches the textbook 8-10 mM citrate range. Was MISSING from FooDB profile.

components.name: orotic acid
components.source: Larson & Hegarty / PMID:1958838 (mean 0.618 mmol/L ≈ 70-95 mg/L); J Dairy Sci review mean 69-74 mg/L
components.evidence: Characteristic bovine-milk pyrimidine intermediate (40-100 mg/L). Was MISSING from FooDB profile.

components.name: L-lactate (lactic acid)
components.source: Fox & McSweeney, Dairy Chemistry; fresh pasteurized milk 0-0.4 g/L
components.evidence: Low in fresh pasteurized milk (present in current profile); rises steeply with microbial fermentation.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: ~80% casein, ~20% whey (beta-lactoglobulin, alpha-lactalbumin). Second-largest N/C source after lactose.

components.name: urea
components.source: FooDB FOOD00891; Dairy Chemistry (milk urea-N ~10-15 mg/dL)
components.evidence: Main non-protein nitrogen fraction of milk.

components.name: pentadecanoic acid (C15:0)
components.evidence: Odd-chain marker of ruminant milk fat.

components.name: cholesterol
components.source: USDA FDC SR Legacy NDB 01079 (8 mg/100g)
components.evidence: GENUINE in milk (animal food) — not noise; 8 mg/100g.

components.name: phosphatidylcholine (milk phospholipids, total)
components.source: FooDB FOOD00891; milk phospholipid ~0.2-0.4 g/L, PC ~30% of MFGM PL
components.evidence: Real milk-fat-globule-membrane phospholipid; individual PC species listed under keep_uncovered.

components.name: Phosphate (phosphorus)
components.evidence: P as inorganic + colloidal calcium phosphate + phosphoserine.

components.name: Chloride
components.source: FooDB FOOD00891; Dairy Chemistry (milk chloride ~1 g/L)

components.name: Sulfate
components.evidence: Minor anion / S source (also via Cys/Met).

components.name: Iodine
components.source: McCance & Widdowson; UK/EU dairy iodine ~10-20 ug/100g (not in USDA panel)
components.evidence: Milk is a major dietary iodine source; feed/season dependent.

components.name: Molybdate (molybdenum)
components.evidence: Ultratrace essential cofactor metal; retained at null-ish trace.

components.name: Riboflavin (B2)
components.evidence: Milk is a rich riboflavin source.

components.name: Niacin (B3, nicotinate)
components.evidence: Preformed niacin low; tryptophan supplies more.

components.name: Cobalamin (B12)
components.evidence: Milk is a good B12 source.

components.name: Myo-inositol
components.source: FooDB FOOD00891; Dairy Chemistry (~0.11 g/L free inositol)
components.evidence: Free sugar-alcohol growth factor in milk.

components.name: Taurine
components.evidence: Free amino-sulfonic acid, trace in bovine milk.

components.name: Creatine
components.evidence: Genuine non-protein N compound (was in uncovered list).

remove.name: Arsenate
remove.reason: toxic trace contaminant, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: Cadmium
remove.reason: toxic trace metal contaminant, not a nutrient

remove.name: Rubidium
remove.reason: non-nutritive trace element, not characteristic of milk

remove.name: Boron
remove.reason: non-nutritive ultratrace element, not a defined milk nutrient

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: ultratrace, not a defined milk growth nutrient (FooDB noise)

remove.name: Fluoride
remove.reason: ultratrace contaminant, not a growth nutrient of milk

remove.name: Sucrose C12H22O11
remove.reason: not a milk sugar; milk carbohydrate is lactose — FooDB noise

remove.name: Maltose C12H22O11
remove.reason: not a milk sugar; FooDB noise

remove.name: D-Fructose
remove.reason: not a characteristic milk sugar; FooDB noise (milk free sugars are glucose/galactose, not fructose)

remove.name: D-Galactarate
remove.reason: mucic acid — biologically implausible in fresh milk; FooDB predicted

remove.name: Acetone
remove.reason: trace volatile ketone (ketosis marker), not a nutrient

remove.name: Methanol
remove.reason: trace volatile, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: 3-Methylbutanoic acid
remove.reason: trace volatile flavor compound, not a quantitatively relevant nutrient

remove.name: L Kynurenine C10H12N2O3
remove.reason: trace tryptophan-catabolite metabolite, not a growth nutrient

remove.name: N-acetyl-L-cysteine
remove.reason: trace/predicted metabolite, not a defined milk nutrient

missing_added: citric acid (~9.4 mM, the principal milk organic acid — was absent)
orotic acid (~70 mg/L characteristic bovine pyrimidine — was absent)
quantitative lactose as the dominant sugar (~4.8 g/100g, 144.7 mM)
free glucose and free galactose amounts
full amino-acid profile with g/100g amounts
full mineral amounts incl. selenium, iodine, chloride
keep_uncovered: phosphatidylcholine species PC(16:0/18:1), PC(18:0/16:1), PC(14:0/18:0), etc. (genuine MFGM phospholipids, no single BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy 'Milk, reduced fat, fluid, 2% milkfat, with added vitamin A and vitamin D' (NDB 01079)
Garnsworthy et al., 'Variability of citric acid in cow milk composition', doi:10.5555/20123239825
Orotic acid in cow milk, PMID:1958838 (mean 0.618 mmol/L)
Fox & McSweeney, Dairy Chemistry and Biochemistry (free sugars, chloride, inositol, urea, carnitine)
McCance & Widdowson / UK dairy iodine reference
notes: Dominant carbon/energy source is LACTOSE (~4.8 g/100g, 144.7 mM); milk protein (~3.3 g, casein+whey) is the main N source. The key correction versus the FooDB profile is adding the milk organic acids — citrate (~9.4 mM, matches textbook 8-10 mM) and orotic acid — and replacing non-milk sugars (sucrose/maltose/fructose noise) with quantified lactose plus trace free glucose/galactose. Cholesterol is genuine (animal food) and kept; toxic/ultratrace metals removed.
